observations.recommendation: We therefore recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Accounting and Treasury Office to reconcile their records at least quarterly. to avoid accumulation of variance.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the details of the balances of Unexpended LDRRMF as shown in Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-002 for fair presentation and to enhance the understandability of the Financial Statements.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive implement austerity measures to prioritize the allocation of budget for terminal leave benefits that are due and demandable. This should be incorporated into the municipality’s annual budget preparation to ensure compliance with Republic Act No. 10154 and to avoid delays in the payment of benefits to retired or separated employees.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive implement measures to expedite project completion and liquidation, addressing operational inefficiencies and enforcing responsibility for delays of projects. Further, impose liquidated damages on the claims of the contractor for failure to satisfactorily complete the work within the specified contract time, without justifiable cause.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Submit to the Audit Team a Sangunian Bayan Ordinance ratifying the various projects amounting to ₱1,935,213.96, and henceforth, ensure to secure prior approval or authorization from the local sanggunian for those projects/programs/activities described in generic terms or in lump-sum appropriation pursuant to Supreme Court En Banc Decision G.R. No. 175527 dated December 8, 2008;
Ensure that all projects appropriated under the 20% Development fund are clearly specified in the Annual Investment Fund; and
Ensure that all specific projects implemented out of the line item described in generic term are among the listed projects in the LDIP.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive ensure that all projects funded under 20% Development Fund contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the Local Government Unit (LGU) in accordance with DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2020-1.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive prioritize adequate and timely procurement planning, especially for foreseeable and recurring activities such as the installation of Christmas decorations and adhere to the provisions of Republic Act No. 9184 (Government Procurement Reform Act) and its Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR), particularly on the prohibition against splitting of contracts.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive submit the lacking documents detailed in Annex B, and henceforth, ensure that all disbursements for infrastructure projects procured through public bidding are accompanied by complete and appropriate supporting documents, in strict compliance with the requirements enumerated under Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive:
Transfer fund equivalent to ₱265,184.87 to the Special Trust Fund out of the General Fund to compensate the funds used from unrelated DRRM purposes; and
Strictly observe Section 5 of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive:
Strictly adhere to the allowable SEF expenditure items outlined in the Joint Circular; and
Ensure that all procured property equipment intended for school use should be properly documented and turned over to the Department of Education, with corresponding Property Transfer Receipts

We recommended that Local Chief Executive:
Ensure that Gender and Development (GAD) Accomplishment Report reflect accurately the actual implementation of the GAD activities during the subject year;
Ensure that during the preparation of GAD Plan and Budget, priority gender issues should be accurately identified in order to avoid augmentation of fund; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer to liquidate the cash advance for the conduct of Gender Responsive Trainings, Seminar, Workshops and Continuing Professional Education in order for the auditor to assess if the said activity is a GAD Responsive Activity.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) Chairperson submit the Gender and Development (GAD) Plan and Budget within five working days from its approval and the GAD Accomplishment Report within five working days from the end of January of the preceding year.

In light of the foregoing, we recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Establish and strengthen the gender-responsive monitoring and evaluation (M&E) system by issuing the necessary Executive or Administrative Order and formally constituting the GAD M&E Team;
Through the monitoring and evaluation (M&E) system, conduct regular assessments of institutional mechanisms for gender mainstreaming to monitor the effectiveness and efficiency of GAD implementation; and
Prepare and ensure the timely submission of the annual LGU Status Report on Institutional Mechanisms for Gender Mainstreaming to the DILG Regional Office.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to strengthen internal controls by ensuring that all future engagements of job order personnel are covered with duly executed contracts and direct the six (6) JOs in question to appear before the audit team and to refund the compensation received on January 22,2025.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the above-mentioned SB officials to refund immediately the overpayment of RATA totaling ₱153,090.00. Henceforth, give only a proportionate amount of RATA based on the actual services rendered.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the municipal assessor to prepare a financial and operational plan and requirements to carry out the tax mapping; and
Enjoin the Sangguniang Bayan to support funding initiatives for the preparation of tax maps and subsequent related activities thereto.

observations.recommendation:
We therefore recommended that management undertake the following remedial measures:

Enjoin the Acting Municipal Treasurer to update her record regularly to facilitate reconciliation of records with the Accounting Office;
Enjoin the Office of the Accounting and the Treasury to reconcile their records regularly to avoid accumulation of variance; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to revise the bank reconciliation and draw adjusting entries to correct the cash in bank balances.

We recommended that management:

Enjoin the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to reconcile their records in order to come up with the correct balance of unexpended DRRMF and to transfer to the Special Trust Fund the remaining balance. However, if found after reconciliation that there was an over-transfer to the Special Trust Fund, replenish the General Fund; and
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure transfer of cash to special trust fund account the total amount of unexpended LDRRMF at year-end.

We recommended that management:

Direct the Human Resource Officer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records to come up with the accurate Leave Benefits Payable;
Enjoin the Sanggunian to fully fund the terminal leave benefits, it being a statutory obligation. Tap the savings from programmed or allotted appropriation which remain free of any obligation or encumbrance; and
Ensure strict compliance with Section 2 of RA 10154.

We reiterated our recommendations that management:

Enjoin all Municipal Collectors to remit all collections intact daily or not later than the next banking day to the Municipal Treasurer; and
Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to monitor daily the remittances of collections of all collecting officers.

We recommended that management:

Instruct the Department Head concern to send a written request to the Source Agencies (SAs) for a copy of the MOA for the subject fund transfers; and
Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to return to the Source Agencies the unexpended/unutilized balances in the Due to NGA account and henceforth, ensure that unspent funds should be returned to the source agency after completion of the project.

We recommended that the management require the concerned offices to create/assign a separate Responsibility Center (RC) for GFPS to facilitate the streamlined report generation pertaining to GAD-related expenses and other financial transactions, or in the alternative, maintain subsidiary ledgers for GAD for each account when it is impractical to establish a separate RC.
We recommended that management provide additional manpower in the BAC Office to assist the BAC Secretariat in all documentation requirements for procurement.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:

If the agency does not have General Services Office, the LGU should assign somebody to man the preparation and monitoring of the said reports.

The person assigned should then verify monthly the official travels, make sure that all trip tickets are properly and fully filled up.

The person assigned must submit the reports (forms attached) to the Auditor as required in the regulation to determine the reasonableness of fuel consumed during the month; otherwise, transactions for fuel and oil for CY 2020 shall be suspended in audit
observations.managementAction: No actions taken.
observations.reason: AAR 2019

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality cease to make advance payments to suppliers for contracts that are not eligible for advance payment. Henceforth, management should ensure that  before making any payment, prior acceptance and inspection of purchased goods have already been made.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that management should enjoin all municipal collecting officers to remit all collections intact daily or not later than the next banking day. Moreover, management should ensure that fidelity bonds of its officers are renewed on a timely basis to avoid this circumstance from reoccurring in the future.

We recommended that management, in relation with the recommendations under AOM No. 2021-012-(20-21):
Implement measures to ensure prompt reporting, remittance, and deposit of collections in order to properly reflect the correct cash balances in the financial statements; and
Establish a proper cutoff in the reporting of collections, especially in the preparation of RCDs, to make sure that collections have been recognized and reported in the correct reporting period.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Reclassify the PPE items in accordance with the criteria set under Item 4.2 of COA Circular No. 2020-006, prepare the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to make necessary adjusting entries to effect the dropping of the PPE items costing below ₱15,000.00, and provide a copy of the JEVs to the Property Custodian together with the schedule or list of affected PPE items for proper reconciliation of records, if there are any; and
Adjust the books of accounts by dropping the road maintenance project from the Other Infrastructure Asset account and submit the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher to the Audit Team.

We recommended strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013. Henceforth, monitoring reports for the Municipality’s PPAs containing details as to project name, location, total cost, date started, extensions, target and actual completion dates, project status and total cost incurred to date, should disclose comprehensively all PPAs that are being implemented by the Municipality.

We recommended that management submit to the audit team copies of the contracts/purchase orders and their supporting documents for the aforementioned projects to permit the conduct of auditorial and technical review.

We recommended that the management to enjoin the MDRRM Officer to submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
We recommended that the Municipality cease to charge unrelated expenditures to the LDRRMF, taking into consideration the existing rules and regulations governing the utilization of the said fund.

We recommended that the management direct all concerned officials to properly and completely accomplish relevant procurement documents such as PR, RFQ, and PO and provide the necessary and relevant specifications of the items to be procured in clear and unambiguous terms for all future procurement projects to serve as reference during inspection and acceptance of deliveries by management.

We recommended that the management:
In congruence to AOM No. 2022-001-(21), ensure that all necessary documentary requirements are submitted by the suppliers; and
Ensure that taxes are properly deducted and withheld before making any payment to suppliers in order to avoid the payment of penalties to the BIR.

We recommended that management strengthen internal control over disbursements after payment or release of check by marking/stamping the disbursement voucher including the supporting documents “PAID” to avoid double payments thru recycling of documents.

We recommended that management explain in writing the aforementioned lapses. Moreover, management should provide this office a proper justification on the conduct of alternative modes of procurement. Henceforth, procurements of the Municipality should be conducted through public bidding as mandated under R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR, unless justified by the conditions provided under Rule XVI of the IRR. In all instances, the procuring entity shall ensure that the most advantageous price for the LGU is obtained.

Require the Accounting Office to record first the purchases of drugs, medicines, and other related supplies as inventory; 

Require the RHU Inventory In-Charge to submit the monthly report of issuances to the Municipal Accountant as basis for the recording of expenses for inventories consumed or distributed; and

Direct the RHU Inventory In-Charge to prepare and update the stock cards regularly and reconcile it with the results of physical inventory count at least on a semestral basis.

Require the Accounting Office to effect adjustments on the PPE account relative to the purchase of the generator set.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the management require the conduct of physical inventory of PPE as required by existing regulations and submit the physical inventory reports to support the book balances of reported PPE; and

Direct the General Services Officer or the Local Treasurer, as the case maybe, to maintain Property Cards per category of PPE and the Accounting Office to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for each type of PPE based on available records/supporting documents.

We have recommended that the management direct the BAC and BAC Secretariat to immediately submit the lacking contracts and supporting documents enumerated in Section 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 of the above-listed projects; otherwise the transactions covered by the unsubmitted documents will be suspended in audit.

We have recommended that the management:

If the agency does not have General Services Office, the LGU should assign somebody to man the preparation and monitoring of the said reports.

The person assigned should then verify monthly the official travels, make sure that all trip tickets are properly and fully filled up.

The person assigned must submit the reports (forms attached) to the Auditor as required in the regulation to determine the reasonableness of fuel consumed during the month; otherwise, transactions for fuel and oil for CY 2020 shall be suspended in audit

We have recommended that management direct the collectors to remit intact all collections at the end of each business day to prevent any justify liability arising from possible losses resulting from unlawful use or application thereof, and for all losses attributable to negligence in keeping of the funds; and

Closely monitor collections that are to be remitted per fund by daily preparation of Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) and updating the Cashbooks.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s Report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to provide the necessary and required specifications on the items to be procured to meet the needs of the Procuring Entity in clear and unambiguous terms for future similar procurement activities.

We recommended that management instruct the MDRRMO thru coordination with concerned municipal officials to submit to the OCD and the COA a complete list of donations received and distributed by the municipality.
We recommended that management enjoin all Municipal Collectors to remit all collections intact daily or not later than the next banking day.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountable Officers to prepare the RAAF and the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the CRAAF and submit the said monthly reports of accountability for accountable forms in the prescribed form and submit the same not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month to the audit team.

We recommended that management:
Require the Accounting Office to record first the purchases of drugs, medicines, and other related supplies as inventory;
Require the RHU Inventory In-Charge to submit the monthly report of issuances to the Municipal Accountant as basis for the recording of expenses for inventories consumed or distributed; and
Direct the RHU Inventory In-Charge to prepare and update the stock cards regularly and reconcile it with the results of physical inventory count at least on a semestral basis.
Require the Accounting Office to effect adjustments on the PPE account relative to the purchase of the generator set.

We recommended that management require the Accounting and Treasury Offices to follow the abovementioned procedures in handling and recording of unreleased checks in order to avoid misstatements related to this in the future.

We recommended that management to minimize the use of reimbursement as a mode of payment for purchases and to resort to any applicable alternative methods of procurement as provided in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 whenever circumstances permit.
We recommended that management, through the Municipal Accountant, submit the aforementioned DVs and supporting documents.

We have recommended that the management to ensure that disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents are properly accomplished and documented and to maintain a healthy control environment to protect municipal resources.

We have recommended that the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the lacking original official receipts (ORs) and/or sales invoices (SIs) for the applicable disbursement vouchers to facilitate the prompt and timely verification of the involved transactions.

We recommend that management instruct the municipal accountant to record the Leave Benefits Payable. Likewise, we reiterate our prior year’s audit recommendation that management strictly comply with the provisions of RA 10154 and its IRR and give the highest priority to the payment and/or settlement of the retirees’ pensions, gratuities and/or other retirement benefits.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant booked Leave Benefits Payable

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor being Head of the Local Development Council as vested under Section 106(b) of RA 7160 (Local Government Code of 1991) shall initiate, formulate and prioritize with the assistance of council members and coordination with the local sanggunian its social and economic development plan including environmental concerns and the Annual Investment Plan in consonance with the prescribed guidelines on the use or utilization of the 20% share of IRA issued by the DILG and DBM with the end view of setting and ensuring its direction towards the realization of desirable socio-economic benefits vis-à-vis poverty alleviation and economic well-being of the constituents.

We have recommended that management make representation to seek assistance from GAD Resource Persons, such as those accredited by the Phil. Commission on Women, for the conduct or participation of a training and workshop on GAD Planning, with emphasis on the use of GAD tools and;

Improve and develop the existing GAD Database or Sex-Disaggregated Data for proper utilization in the planning, budgeting, programming, and policy formulation of the Municipality, as well as proper charging of the object of expenditures on actual accomplishments.

We have recommended that the management shall enforce the accounting unit to carefully check the completeness of the documentary requirements of all disbursements as prescribed in COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012; and
Direct the HR Officer to update monthly the employees’ leave cards in order to come up with the correct balances per month.

observations.reason: The Municipality have already filled the position for the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:

Direct the HRMO to execute contract for employees hired under JO to include statement of duties and responsibilities as a means by which their performance can be measured;

Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the SP authorization/resolution, contract of services/job orders and Accomplishment Report for every paid payroll; and

Strictly observe Section 4 (6) of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular 2012-001 on government documentary requirements.

We have reiterated our recommendation that management require the conduct of physical inventory of PPE as required by existing regulations and submit the physical inventory reports to support the book balances of reported PPE; and

Direct the General Services Officer or the Local Treasurer, as the case maybe, to maintain Property Cards per category of PPE and the Accounting Office to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for each type of PPE based on available records/supporting documents.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor being Head of the Local Development Council as vested under Section 106(b) of RA 7160 (Local Government Code of 1991) shall initiate, formulate and prioritize with the assistance of council members and coordination with the local sanggunian its social and economic development plan including environmental concerns and the Annual Investment Plan in consonance with the prescribed guidelines on the use or utilization of the 20% share of IRA issued by the DILG and DBM with the end view of setting and ensuring its direction towards the realization of desirable socio-economic benefits vis-à-vis poverty alleviation and economic well-being of the constituents.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the management shall enforce the accounting unit to carefully check the completeness of the documentary requirements of all disbursements as prescribed in COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012; and
Direct the HR Officer to update monthly the employees’ leave cards in order to come up with the correct balances per month.
observations.managementAction:
The following documentary requirements need to be checked by the accounting office before approving leave Monitization Application; 
Filling up leave application 
Certification from the HRMO Officer as to the correctness of the applied number of days to be monetized
Leave cards showing leave balances
Approved vouchers
Procurement thru canvass & negotiated contract to comply the requirements

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to management that if the LGU does not have General Services Officer, it should specifically assign somebody to man the preparation and monitoring of the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and the Monthly Report of Official Travel. The person assigned should verify monthly the official travels and make sure that all trip tickets are properly and completely filled up.

Prepare and submit the reports to the Auditor as required in the regulation to determine the reasonableness of fuel consumed during the month

We have reiterated our recommendation to management to:
Require the municipal accountant to retrieve pertinent vouchers/payrolls and, where necessary, prepare the reclassifying and/or correcting entries;
Set-up and maintain Subsidiary Ledgers showing detailed information on cash advances granted and liquidations per accountable officer/employee; and
Request for authority to write-off dormant unliquidated cash advances which remained non-moving for ten (10) years or more and where settlement could no longer be ascertained in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We have recommended that management:
Require the conduct of physical inventory of PPE as required by existing regulations and submit the physical inventory reports to support the book balances of reported PPE; and
Direct the General Services Officer or the Local Treasurer, as the case maybe, to maintain Property Cards per category of PPE and the Accounting Office to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for each type of PPE based on available records/supporting documents.

We recommend that management instruct the municipal accountant to record the Leave Benefits Payable. Likewise, we reiterate our prior year’s audit recommendation that management strictly comply with the provisions of RA 10154 and its IRR and give the highest priority to the payment and/or settlement of the retirees’ pensions, gratuities and/or other retirement benefits.

We have recommended that management direct the BAC and BAC Secretariat to immediately submit the lacking contracts and supporting documents enumerated in Section 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 of the above-listed projects; otherwise the transactions covered by the unsubmitted documents will be suspended in audit.

We have recommended that Management:
If the agency does not have General Services Office, the LGU should assign somebody to man the preparation and monitoring of the said reports.
The person assigned should then verify monthly the official travels, make sure that all trip tickets are properly and fully filled up.
The person assigned must submit the reports (forms attached) to the Auditor as required in the regulation to determine the reasonableness of fuel consumed during the month; otherwise, transactions for fuel and oil for CY 2020 shall be suspended in audit
We have reiterated our recommendation that management direct the Municipal Accountant to give utmost priority in the preparation of monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements; and

We have recommended that management discontinue the practice of granting cash advances to the DO without the necessary supporting documents such as approved payrolls, estimate of expenses,  obligation requests and certification of proper officials; and

Grant cash advance only for a legally specific purpose. The specific purpose shall be indicated in the appropriate box in the DV.

We have recommended that management direct the Accounting Unit to provide the necessary documentary requirements of the 70 post-audited disbursement vouchers to avoid suspension of the transactions.   For the succeeding disbursements, examine thoroughly the completeness of the documentary requirements for every transaction before approving the same.

We have recommended that management make representation to seek assistance from GAD Resource Persons, such as those accredited by the Phil. Commission on Women, for the conduct or participation of a training and workshop on GAD Planning, with emphasis on the use of GAD tools and;

Improve and develop the existing GAD Database or Sex-Disaggregated Data for proper utilization in the planning, budgeting, programming, and policy formulation of the Municipality, as well as proper charging of the object of expenditures on actual accomplishments.
observations.managementAction:
Creation of G.F.P.S thru the issuance of E.O.
Prepare an activity design for the conduct of HDGD Training for the GFPS
Seek approval from the LCE.
Make a letter requesting for the assistance for the GAD resource person
observations.reason: Focal Person attended the seminar but HGDG is yet to be applied in CY 2020 GAD Plan

Henceforth, all government contracts and their supporting documents shall be submitted to the Commission as prescribed in the said Circular within five (5) calendar days from the execution of a contract thereof.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to Management that:

The GAD Focal Point is advised to comply with the pertinent provisions of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 on the preparation of Annual GAD Plans and Budgets, and Accomplishment Reports. This circular provides clearer guidelines and procedures in the formulation, development, submission, implementation, monitoring and evaluation including accounting of results of the agency annual GAD plans and budgets and GAD accomplishment reports. Likewise, it provides the mechanics for the development of programs, activities and projects to respect, protect and fulfil the rights of women at the socio-cultural, economic and political spheres;

The LGU should submit the GPB to the PPDO of the Provincial Government to ensure that activities to be undertaken are part of the GAD priority agenda of the provincial government and from PPDO to the DILG Regional Office for review and approval; and

The Accounting Office should prepare and observe timely submission to COA of GAD Expenditures duly certified by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.managementAction: Prepared and submitted 2017 GAD Plan and Budget to DILG
observations.reason: The Municipality already submitted the 2017 GAD Plan and Budget to DILG and has been favorably reviewed.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:
1.	Direct the Municipal Accountant to give utmost priority in the preparation of monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements.

2.	Direct the Municipal Accountant to closely supervise the accounting personnel tasked to prepare the Monthly Reconciliation Statements.

3.	Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to conduct monthly reconciliation of the cash accounts to facilitate smooth preparation of the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements.
observations.managementAction: Prepared and submitted Bank Reconciliation Statements. Bank statements were not received on time, hence the late submission.
observations.reason: BRS were already submitted, however the preparation was improper.

observations.recommendation:
To strictly maintain and update Subsidiary Ledger and Property Card for each item of PPE.

To strictly conduct an actual physical inventory-taking of all PPE acquired at least once a year through the Inventory Committee.

To strictly prepare Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment in accordance with the NGAS Manual.

To properly place Property Identification Card/ Property Tag to each item of PPE.

To prepare an Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) every time a PPE item is acquired.

To send to actual trainings and seminars the Property Officer/Municipal Treasurer regarding the keeping, maintenance and disposition of PPE for proper guidance in property management.

To properly install strong internal control in safeguarding the government’s properties.
observations.managementAction: The LCE is still to designate a Property Custodian.
observations.reason:
No Subsidiary Ledger and Property card was prepared.

No actual physical inventory was conducted.

No report on the Physical Count of PPE was prepared.

No Property Tagging was done.

No ARE was prepared.

No strong internal control was employed

We have recommended to Management that:
The GAD Focal Point is advised to comply with the pertinent provisions of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 on the preparation of Annual GAD Plans and Budgets, and Accomplishment Reports. This circular provides clearer guidelines and procedures in the formulation, development, submission, implementation, monitoring and evaluation including accounting of results of the agency annual GAD plans and budgets and GAD accomplishment reports. Likewise, it provides the mechanics for the development of programs, activities and projects to respect, protect and fulfil the rights of women at the socio-cultural, economic and political spheres;
The LGU should submit the GPB to the PPDO of the Provincial Government to ensure that activities to be undertaken are part of the GAD priority agenda of the provincial government and from PPDO to the DILG Regional Office for review and approval; and
The Accounting Office should prepare and observe timely submission to COA of GAD Expenditures duly certified by the Municipal Accountant.

We have recommended that the municipal treasurer and the municipal accountant should exert all efforts to locate the records/documents for analysis/review of accounts and if not possible due to its absence, transfer said accounts to the Registry Book of Dormant Account for the meantime.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to give utmost priority in the preparation of monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to closely supervise the accounting personnel tasked to prepare the Monthly Reconciliation Statements.

Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to conduct monthly reconciliation of the cash accounts to facilitate smooth preparation of the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements.
observations.reason: BRS for the General Fund was not submitted from June to December 2016.

observations.recommendation:
The Local Chief Executive (LCE) shall determine the LGU properties not yet covered by insurance and recommend to the local legislative body the amount necessary for insurance coverage for appropriation.

The local legislative body shall include in the appropriation ordinance of the municipality the amount of necessary for the insurance coverage of LGU properties as recommended by the LCE.

The Property Officer shall facilitate the application with the GSIS of an insurance coverage of all its properties that the municipality has an insurable interest to insure that any loss or damage to the same will be indemnified by the insurer.
observations.managementAction: With insurance coverage for vehicles
observations.reason: Property, Plant and Equipment, except vehicles, were still not covered by an insurance from the GSIS.

observations.recommendation:
Transfer at year-end the unexpended balance of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liability-DRRM” in the Trust Fund books pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002. 

Maintain a separate registry of appropriations, allotments and obligations for the LDRRMF (a) RAAO- for QRF,(b) RAAO- for MOOE and (c) RAAO- for CO, for easy determination of the unexpended balance of the fund.

The Municipal Accountant should immediately submit the required reports and accounts and to strictly observe the pertinent provision of PD No. 1445 and Section 7.2.1 of the 2009 Rules & Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts. 

Augment personnel in the accounting and treasury office to handle the preparation and submission of the reports.
observations.managementAction: No concrete action was undertaken.
observations.reason: Management endeavors to submit the financial reports but still incurs a considerable delay.
